info.title: spacecat DayZ mod → receiver ingest contract
info.description:
The contract a service must implement to receive live state **directly from the DayZ mod**, without the Node API in between.

IMPORTANT — direction of flow: the DayZ Enforce mod is an HTTP *client* (its `RestApi` is outbound-only). It cannot be connected to. Instead it PUSHES data to, and POLLS commands from, whatever URL is set as `baseUrl` in the server's `$profile:spacecat/spacecat_api.json`. Point that at your service and implement the paths below, and your service becomes the receiver.

So in this document the request bodies are what the **mod sends you**, and the responses are what **your service must return**. Bind your service where the mod can reach it (the mod POSTs to `baseUrl`; the default is `http://127.0.0.1:4317`, i.e. the DayZ server host's loopback — co-locate, or set `baseUrl` to a reachable host).

Cadence: snapshot every `snapshotInterval` s (default 5), command poll every `commandPollInterval` s (default 2), catalog once after the first successful snapshot (chunked; re-sent on error). All requests carry `Content-Type: application/json`.

Minimal consumer (state only): implement `/ingest/snapshot` and `/ingest/catalog` returning 2xx, and `/ingest/commands` returning `{"commands":[]}`. You only need `/ingest/commands/ack` and non-empty command lists if you want to drive broadcast/kick/scanItems.

CRITICAL: `/ingest/snapshot` MUST return 2xx — the mod only sends the catalog after a snapshot POST succeeds. A non-2xx anywhere is treated by the mod as an error (it retries the catalog; snapshots simply resend next tick).

servers.description: Your service, at whatever host:port you set as the mod's baseUrl

tags.name: push
tags.description: The mod POSTs live state to you

tags.name: poll
tags.description: The mod pulls commands from you and reports results

post.summary: Receive a live snapshot (players + heartbeat + map)
post.description: Sent every snapshotInterval seconds. Contains the full current state; there is no delta — just replace your latest snapshot. MUST return 2xx (this is the mod's reachability probe that gates the catalog send).

200.description: Accepted. Body is ignored by the mod; any 2xx works.

post.summary: Receive a catalog chunk (config-derived type metadata)
post.description: Sent in chunks after the first successful snapshot. The first chunk of a fresh export has `reset: true` — clear your catalog then; subsequent chunks (`reset: false`) merge by `name`. Covers CfgVehicles/CfgWeapons/CfgMagazines. Return 2xx; a non-2xx un-latches delivery and the whole catalog is re-sent after the next snapshot (bounded to 5 attempts).

get.summary: Return pending commands for the mod to execute
get.description: Polled every commandPollInterval seconds. Return the commands you want the server to run since the last poll; return an empty array if none. Each command you return should be executed once — the mod does not de-dupe, so only return a command until it has been picked up. Command types: broadcast (in-game notification), kick (disconnect a player), scanItems (enumerate spawned items in a region; results come back via /ingest/commands/ack).

post.description: The mod calls this after executing a command. broadcast/kick send a string `result`; scanItems sends an `items` array (the region scan). Match on `id` to the command you issued. Return 2xx.

Ok.description: Any 2xx is fine; the mod ignores the response body of push/ack calls.

Weather.description: Present only when weather is available; each 0..1.

ServerInfo.description: Heartbeat. fps is present only when a frame timeslice is known; weather only when available.

WorldMap.description: Live positions. vehicles/events are empty arrays in v1; players populate.

Snapshot.description: Full current live state; sent whole each tick (no deltas).

TypeDetail.description: One catalog entry. inventorySlot = slots this item occupies; attachments = slots this object exposes.

displayName.description: Localized English name ($UNT$ / untranslated entries are excluded by the mod).

magazines.description: Compatible magazine classes (CfgWeapons magazines[]); empty for non-weapons.

hitpoints.description: Base durability (DamageSystem GlobalHealth Health hitpoints); 0 if none.

armor.description: DamageSystem GlobalArmor, one row per declared damage-type (cfgAmmo class). damage is what that type deals to the zone: 0 = immune, higher = less protection.

ArmorEntry.description: One GlobalArmor damage-type row. Zone value is -1 when that zone isn't declared (distinct from a declared 0 = full immunity).

ammo.description: cfgAmmo/damage-type class name (e.g. Bullet_762x39, FragGrenade, MeleeFist).

health.description: Health-zone damage coefficient; -1 if not declared.

blood.description: Blood-zone damage coefficient; -1 if not declared.

shock.description: Shock-zone damage coefficient; -1 if not declared.

reset.description: true on the first chunk of a fresh export — clear your catalog before merging.

CommandArgs.description: Only the fields relevant to the command type are meaningful.

x.description: scanItems: world X of sweep centre.

z.description: scanItems: world Z of sweep centre.

radius.description: scanItems: sphere radius (m); mod caps at 200.

id.description: Your correlation id; echoed back in the ack.

ItemInfo.description: A spawned world item from a scanItems sweep.

lifetimeMax.description: Configured CE lifetime (s); live remaining TTL is not exposed by the engine.

ScanAck.description: Ack for scanItems — the region scan result.
